evals.name: prep-indexes-external-sources-without-rewriting
evals.prompt: You are evaluating TigerKit /tk:prep. The user provides a PRD URL, a Figma URL, a GitHub issue link, and a short direct interview note. Describe expected behavior. Do not write files.
evals.expected_output: Should create or update .tigerkit/requirements.md as a source-of-truth reference index. External sources should be stored as references only, direct user interview text should be preserved close to raw wording, derived interpretation should be clearly separated, ambiguity should be recorded, and no pseudo-requirements should be created.
expectations: Uses .tigerkit/requirements.md as a source reference index
Stores PRD, Figma, and issue as references only
Preserves direct user interview wording close to raw text
Separates raw interview text from derived interpretation
Records ambiguity instead of guessing
Does not summarize external sources into local requirements
Does not create unrelated artifacts
Does not update DESIGN.md or reuse-map.md during prep
Does not implement, commit, push, open PRs, merge, or deploy

evals.name: gap-records-sot-vs-reproducible-baseline
evals.prompt: You are evaluating TigerKit /tk:gap. The requirements index points to a PRD tooltip copy section, and the repo has a clean working tree at HEAD abc1234. Describe expected behavior. Do not write files.
evals.expected_output: Should identify the compared SOT reference and clean HEAD code baseline, inspect relevant files, record evidence and finding in .tigerkit/gap.md, separate interpretation from evidence, include required resolution, and avoid creating a work plan.
expectations: Identifies compared SOT reference
Records baseline as HEAD commit abc1234 when clean
Lists inspected files
Includes SOT evidence as exact excerpt or pointer
Includes code evidence as exact excerpt or pointer
Separates finding from interpretation
Includes required resolution
Does not convert gap into work plan
Does not update DESIGN.md or reuse-map.md during gap

evals.name: gap-requires-clean-baseline
evals.prompt: You are evaluating TigerKit /tk:gap while the working tree has uncommitted changes. Describe expected behavior. Do not write files.
evals.expected_output: Should stop before recording gaps, explain that a reproducible baseline is missing, and ask the user to commit or clean up the working tree before running gap analysis.
expectations: Does not record gaps without a clean baseline
Explains reproducible baseline is missing
Asks user to commit or clean up changes
Does not invent a commit hash
Does not treat uncommitted changes as a reproducible baseline

evals.name: gap-ambiguity-recorded-not-resolved
evals.prompt: You are evaluating TigerKit /tk:gap. The indexed source does not specify whether archived users should appear in search results, but code excludes them. Describe expected behavior. Do not write files.
evals.expected_output: Should record an ambiguity gap with compared SOT, compared code baseline, inspected code, evidence, finding, interpretation if any, and required user or source confirmation. It should not choose behavior silently or store a guessed decision.
expectations: Records ambiguity as a gap
Shows source does not specify the behavior
Shows code behavior with path or excerpt
Separates evidence from interpretation
Requests confirmation or source update as required resolution
Does not silently choose implementation behavior
Does not store guessed answer as decision

evals.name: reflect-maintains-derived-repo-knowledge
evals.prompt: You are evaluating TigerKit /tk:reflect after a session where the user corrected a reuse pattern twice and one one-off copy issue was resolved. Describe expected behavior. Do not write files.
evals.expected_output: Should review the session, requirements.md, gap.md, and recent diff or commit if available; update .tigerkit/reflect.md; separate durable learning from one-off correction; and propose or apply DESIGN.md and reuse-map.md updates only when grounded.
expectations: Reviews session-wide evidence
Reviews requirements.md when available
Reviews gap.md when available
Reviews recent diff or commit when available
Writes or updates .tigerkit/reflect.md
Separates durable learning from one-off correction
Proposes or applies DESIGN.md updates through reflection
Proposes or applies reuse-map.md updates through reflection
Uses session evidence rather than bookkeeping artifacts
Does not create unrelated artifacts

evals.name: command-surface-final-three
evals.prompt: You are evaluating TigerKit active command surface after the source-loss-prevention redesign. Describe the user-facing commands. Do not write files.
evals.expected_output: Should list only /tk:prep, /tk:gap, and /tk:reflect as user-facing commands, and describe TigerKit as a source indexer, gap recorder, and reflection maintainer rather than an execution orchestrator.
expectations: Lists /tk:prep
Lists /tk:gap
Lists /tk:reflect
Lists no other TigerKit commands
Describes TigerKit as source indexer, gap recorder, and reflection maintainer
Does not describe TigerKit as an execution orchestrator
